_meta.note: Curated, hand-authored crafting recipes for common gear. These are step-by-step guides, NOT solver output, because they use deterministic recipes (rune-forging, fracture-locks, desecration sequences) that fall outside the optimizer's scope. Costs are VOLATILE and given as rough league-snapshot ranges; treat them as estimates, not quotes. Recipes are adapted from public 0.5 (Runes of Aldur) crafting guides and flagged where a step depends on unpublished odds.

low.base: Buy a white (Normal) Quarterstaff, item level 82+, highest %physical-damage implicit you can afford.
steps: Use the optimizer (Step 1-3) for a budget physical + attack-speed staff: it will route you through Transmutation then Augmentation/Regal to add the mods you pick.
Target: one of %increased Physical Damage (prefix) plus Attack Speed (suffix) to start; add Crit or flat phys later if budget allows.
This is the cheapest entry: no essences or desecration, just orbs.

med.base: Buy a white Quarterstaff ilvl 82+ with a high %phys implicit.
steps: Greater Essence of (your element/attack mod) to guarantee one strong prefix and upgrade to Magic.
Augmentation / Greater Augmentation to add a second prefix; Regal to Rare when you have your prefixes.
Use the optimizer with Essence + Greater/Perfect orbs enabled and pick your two prefixes + a suffix; follow its step list.
Finish with Exalted Orbs (or one Exalted + Omen of Greater Exaltation when you have 2 open slots) to fill remaining suffixes.

This high-end staff is NOT made with orbs/essences. It is rune-forged: buy a Warding Starlet Ore (~30 ex) and forge it at the Verisium Anvil to get a Duality quarterstaff (rolls 200-300% phys, 20-30% crit damage, 12-22% attack speed). Re-roll/buy until you get a high physical + high crit-damage roll.

Upgrade it with the rune-master recipe: needs ~20 Exceptional Verisium (~3 ex each), an Oloth's Crest of the Sun (price volatile, was ~120 ex), and the upgrade RECIPE which drops from the Runes of Kings March expedition league mechanic (not from the campaign).
Rune-forging gives one of two outcomes: +50% weapon damage OR +50% crit chance (T1-equivalent crit). Both are excellent.
Because this is deterministic, the optimizer does not compute it. The value is that an equivalent rare staff costs far more than the materials.
high.flag: Deterministic rune-forge recipe; materials and the expedition recipe are required. Costs highly volatile.

low.base: Buy a Magic Obliterator Bow (or similar high-%phys bow) with ~130%+ increased physical damage, ilvl 82+. ~4-5 div for a good roll.
steps: Greater Essence of Abrasion: guarantees flat physical damage and upgrades toward Rare.
If you only need physical + attack speed, use the optimizer with Essence enabled, pick flat-phys + attack-speed, and follow its steps.
Fill the last suffix with a Greater Exalted Orb.

med.base: Magic Obliterator Bow, ~130-150% phys implicit, ilvl 82+, ideally with a good open prefix.
steps: Greater Essence of Abrasion for guaranteed flat physical (prefix 1).

Omen of Sinistral Necromancy + Omen of the Liege + Preserved Jawbone: forces a guaranteed PREFIX desecration locked to the Liege (Amon-Amarth) pool. Reveal at the Well of Souls; the companion-damage / 'increased damage while companion present' mods are guaranteed from this pool.
After your three prefixes are set, use an Omen of Greater Exaltation, then a Greater Exalted Orb, to slam two suffixes at once (saves money vs two exalts when greater exalts are pricey).
Suffixes are random here: aim for +level of gems, attack speed, or other useful rolls.
med.flag: Desecration reveal weights are unpublished; the Liege pool guarantees the mod TYPE, the exact reveal among that pool is RNG.

high.base: High-tier Bow base, item level 82 for top tiers.
steps: Priority mods: flat added elemental damage (for elemental bows) or % increased Physical Damage (for phys bows) (prefixes); attack speed, critical hit chance, +levels to projectile/bow skills, accuracy on phys variants (suffixes). Deadeye/Pathfinder scale projectile attacks; pick the damage type your skill uses.
Start from a high item-level base that already has one or two strong mods.
Target the remaining mods with desecration (Bone + a lord omen on weapons/jewellery) or Expedition, then finish with the optimizer.
Alloys can force one guaranteed mod by replacing a random one. Desecration reveal odds are unpublished, so treat targeting as best-effort, not guaranteed.
high.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
high.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

high.base: Buy a +5 (to spell skills) Wand base, ilvl 81+. Use +5, not +4: the runes are expensive and not worth it on a cheap base. Prefix doesn't matter (it gets removed).
steps: Socket the wand (Artificer) and insert an Astramentis-style +1-crafted-mod rune (Astrid's Creativity) so the item can hold an extra crafted modifier.
Apply a crit-chance-for-spells essence (becomes Rare with your guaranteed mod).
Omen of Sinistral Crystallization + a Transcendent/abyss essence (alloy) to convert toward a prefix while removing an unwanted suffix; this adds the second crafted modifier.
Add a Jawbone desecration for another prefix; use Omen of Abyssal Echoes when revealing and BLOCK mana (take the mana reveal first so exalts later don't all roll mana).
Omen of Greater Exaltation + Perfect Exalted Orb on the two open prefixes; re-roll undesired mods with Omen of Light + Annulment.
Swap the +1-crafted rune for a +1-to-all-spell-skills rune at the very end (replaces the crafted mod cleanly).
high.flag: Uses rune-based metacrafting (+1 crafted mod) and alloys; deterministic-ish but expensive. Not solver-computed.

low.base: Wand with a useful base, item level 82.
steps: Priority mods: +levels to spell or specific-element skills, % increased Spell Damage, flat added spell damage of your element (prefixes); cast speed, spell critical chance / critical damage, mana (suffixes). Stormweaver/Chronomancer scale spells; match the element of your main skill.
Buy a white base at the right item level, or a cheap magic/rare off trade.
Run the optimizer (Steps 1-3) to add your priority mods via Transmutation then Augmentation/Regal.
Socket runes for resistances or life to patch gaps cheaply.
low.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
low.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

med.base: Wand with a useful base, item level 82.
steps: Priority mods: +levels to spell or specific-element skills, % increased Spell Damage, flat added spell damage of your element (prefixes); cast speed, spell critical chance / critical damage, mana (suffixes). Stormweaver/Chronomancer scale spells; match the element of your main skill.
Use an Essence to guarantee your most important mod (or a missing resistance), then the optimizer to add the rest and push tiers.
Socket the best runes you can. For armour, Verisium Runeforging adds Runic Ward (free below item level 55, trades some base defence above it).
med.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot

Buy a white base at the right item level, or a cheap magic/rare off trade.
Run the optimizer (Steps 1-3) to add your priority mods via Transmutation then Augmentation/Regal.
Socket runes for resistances or life to patch gaps cheaply.

low.flag: Warrior/mace is widely rated weak in 0.5. Targets are stable, but the archetype itself underperforms this patch. Verify before investing.

shared_slots._note: Slot recipes that apply across classes (armour pieces use the same mod pools regardless of class; only the attribute requirement of the base differs).

low.base: Buy a white boots base matching your build's defence (evasion / armour / ES / hybrid), ilvl 75+ (82 for T1 resistances). Movement speed is the key suffix you want.
steps: Use a resistance Essence to guarantee a resistance, then the optimizer to add movement speed + a second resistance.
For a budget pair you can just buy a Magic base with 30-35% movement speed and a resistance off trade instead of crafting.

med.base: White boots base (preferably an 'exceptional' double-socket base if cheap, under ~1 div), ilvl 82.
steps: Quality + socket the base FIRST (Putrefaction corrupts the item, so you can't quality/socket afterward).
Greater Essence of (the resistance/attribute you need) to guarantee a useful mod.
Omen of Sinistral/Dextral Necromancy + Preserved Rib to force a prefix/suffix desecration; reveal at the Well of Souls (hold an Omen of Abyssal Echoes for a second reveal on a valuable pair).
Omen of Greater Exaltation + Greater Exalted Orb to fill the remaining mods two at a time.
Target: 35% movement speed, two resistances, and an evasion/ES/life roll matching your defence.
med.flag: Desecration reveal odds unpublished; modeled as RNG in CraftPath's putrefaction estimate.

high.base: Boots matching your build's defence, item level 82 for T1 resistances.
steps: Priority mods: movement speed is the key suffix; life prefix; two resistances. Runeforge Runic Ward if you want a defensive layer.
Start from a high item-level base that already has one or two strong mods.
Target the remaining mods with desecration (Bone + a lord omen on weapons/jewellery) or Expedition, then finish with the optimizer.
Alloys can force one guaranteed mod by replacing a random one. Desecration reveal odds are unpublished, so treat targeting as best-effort, not guaranteed.
high.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
high.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

med.base: Exceptional white helmet base matching your defence, ilvl 82.
steps: Annul + Augment (or Transmute/Augment) to roll two good mods, e.g. T1 resistance + a %defence or flat-defence roll. Avoid the hybrid roll if you want room for rarity.
Use a Rarity essence to guarantee increased rarity (in demand this league) and reach three good mods.
Desecrate for a fourth mod (Rib + necromancy omen), then Divine for the roll if needed.
If it is a rarity-focused league, fracture a key mod on boots/gloves/helmet so you can keep rolling the rest safely.
med.flag: Fracture-and-lock is a manual step; CraftPath does not model fracturing as an optimizer action.

low.base: Helmet matching your build's defence, item level 82.
steps: Priority mods: life and a big defence roll as prefixes; resistances and attributes as suffixes. Some helmets grant +level to a specific skill.
Buy a white base at the right item level, or a cheap magic/rare off trade.
Run the optimizer (Steps 1-3) to add your priority mods via Transmutation then Augmentation/Regal.
Socket runes for resistances or life to patch gaps cheaply.

low.base: White amulet base (Gold amulet is best but ~3x pricier; Solar is a good second). ilvl 75+ for all T1 mods. An open prefix is worth paying for.
steps: Perfect Augmentation if you have an open prefix (or accept a prefix you can live with).
Greater Opulence to guarantee a high rarity tier.
Omen of Sinistral Necromancy + Preserved Collarbone to force a guaranteed PREFIX desecration (the best prefixes are rarity and spirit; even a 'miss' is often a good defensive roll).
Omen of Greater Exaltation + Greater Exalted Orb to slam suffixes (crit + resistances) two at a time.
Acceptable prefixes: any life/ES/evasion, spirit, rarity. Acceptable suffixes: crit, resistances.
low.flag: Jackpot-driven (spirit/rarity desecration hits are high value); desecration reveal odds unpublished.

med.base: White amulet base, ideally with an open prefix, ilvl 82.
steps: Perfect Augmentation on the open prefix.
Greater Opulence to guarantee rarity; Omen of Sinistral Necromancy + Collarbone for a guaranteed prefix desecration.
Fracturing Orb to lock a +3 (to skills) desecrated prefix: with a desecration present you have a ~1-in-3 fracture (the desecrated mod can't be fractured). A fractured +3 amulet is worth a lot.

Add defensive resistance catalysts (fire or lightning, NOT cold; cold's T4 still rolls at the perfect-exalt level-50 floor), then Omen of Catalyzing Exaltation + Perfect Exalted Orb to bias toward resistances.
Quality with catalysts before selling so the displayed numbers look higher.
med.flag: Catalyst weighting multipliers are the creator's own testing, unverified. Fracture is a manual lock step, not solver-modeled.

steps: Priority mods: +skill levels and spirit are the big prefixes; life, resistances, attributes, and crit as suffixes. Desecration/Expedition help land + skill levels.
Start from a high item-level base that already has one or two strong mods.
Target the remaining mods with desecration (Bone + a lord omen on weapons/jewellery) or Expedition, then finish with the optimizer.
Alloys can force one guaranteed mod by replacing a random one. Desecration reveal odds are unpublished, so treat targeting as best-effort, not guaranteed.
high.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
high.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

high.base: Magic Vile Robe (or your defence base) ilvl 82 with T1 %energy-shield. Buy a few copies: a 1-in-3 fracture must succeed.
steps: Artificer-socket the base and slot the new soul-mod rune (e.g. Medvit's Tending) up front so a lucky Perfect Regal can hit the mana/spirit mod.
Perfect Regal Orb hoping for the mana roll (usually misses; that's fine).
Fracture T1 energy shield: put a Preserved Rib desecration on for a 4th mod (it can't be fractured), giving a 1-in-3 to lock the ES. If it fails, start over with a fresh base.
Annul down to one mod, Exalt back to set up, then Chaos-spam to roll the 1-20% mana (or spirit) prefix (no published weights; expect ~250+ chaos on average).
Omen of Sinistral Necromancy + Ancient Rib for a guaranteed prefix desecration -> reveal flat ES (Abyssal Echoes for a 2nd reveal).
Perfect Exalted Orb + Omen of Greater Exaltation for two suffixes; finish, quality, iron rune, Divine the mana roll.
high.flag: Mana/spirit roll has no published weights (creator estimate ~250 chaos avg). One mandatory 1-in-3 fracture. Not solver-computed.

med.base: Ring base; Iron Ring gives a flat-damage implicit useful for attack builds. Item level 82.
steps: Priority mods: life, mana, and flat added damage as prefixes; resistances, attributes, and (where the base allows) crit as suffixes.
Use an Essence to guarantee your most important mod (or a missing resistance), then the optimizer to add the rest and push tiers.
Socket the best runes you can. For armour, Verisium Runeforging adds Runic Ward (free below item level 55, trades some base defence above it).
med.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
med.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

high.base: Ring base; Iron Ring gives a flat-damage implicit useful for attack builds. Item level 82.
steps: Priority mods: life, mana, and flat added damage as prefixes; resistances, attributes, and (where the base allows) crit as suffixes.
Start from a high item-level base that already has one or two strong mods.
Target the remaining mods with desecration (Bone + a lord omen on weapons/jewellery) or Expedition, then finish with the optimizer.
Alloys can force one guaranteed mod by replacing a random one. Desecration reveal odds are unpublished, so treat targeting as best-effort, not guaranteed.
high.source: Inferred from current 0.5 build guides + stable slot priorities; NOT verified best-in-slot
high.flag: Inference, not a published recipe. The 0.5 meta is days old; verify on a current build guide before heavy investment.

desecration.title: Desecration / Well of Souls reference
desecration.intro: Desecration adds an Unrevealed Desecrated modifier that you reveal at the Well of Souls. If the item's affixes are already full, a random existing modifier is removed first. An item that already carries a Desecrated modifier cannot be Desecrated again. A reveal can land a normal base modifier, not only a desecrated one, unless an Omen forces a named (lord) modifier.
currencies: Jawbone (Gnawed / Preserved / Ancient): desecrates a Rare Weapon or Quiver.
Rib (Gnawed / Preserved / Ancient): desecrates a Rare Armour.
Collarbone (Gnawed / Preserved / Ancient): desecrates a Rare Amulet, Ring or Belt.
Preserved Cranium: desecrates a Rare Jewel. Unstable: rising chance to destroy the item each use.
Preserved Vertebrae: desecrates a Rare Waystone.
Altered Collarbone: desecrates a Rare Amulet/Ring/Belt with a chance for otherworldly modifiers.
Bone tier gating: Gnawed = maximum item level 64; Preserved = no cap; Ancient = minimum modifier level 40.
omens: Omen of the Sovereign: next Weapon/Jewellery desecration guarantees a random Ulaman modifier.
Omen of the Liege: next Weapon/Jewellery desecration guarantees a random Amanamu modifier.
Omen of the Blackblooded: next Weapon/Jewellery desecration guarantees a random Kurgal modifier.
Omen of Abyssal Echoes: reroll the reveal options once.
Omen of Putrefaction: replace all modifiers, creating up to 6 Unrevealed modifiers, and Corrupt the item.
Omen of Light: your next Orb of Annulment removes only Desecrated modifiers.
Omen of Sinistral Necromancy: next desecration adds only prefixes.
Omen of Dextral Necromancy: next desecration adds only suffixes.
rules: A reveal may include a normal base modifier unless an omen guarantees a named lord modifier.
Body Armour, Gloves, Boots and Helmet have no prefix desecrated modifiers (suffix-only).
Sceptres have no exclusive desecrated modifiers.
Lich (lord-forcing) omens work only on Weapons and Jewellery.
Jewel exclusive desecrated modifiers roll only on regular jewels, not Time-Lost jewels.
desecration.lords: The three Abyssal Lords are Ulaman, Amanamu and Kurgal; each lord omen guarantees a random modifier from that lord's pool.
desecration.source: PoE2DB desecrated modifier tables + Well of Souls / Abyss notes (0.5 Runes of Aldur)

methods.title: Magic base flipping (white to magic, for resale)
methods.when: Low on currency, or you want to mass-produce sellable magic bases instead of buying them.
methods.base: A white (Normal) base at the right item level. Item level caps the tiers, so buy ilvl 82 bases when you want tier-1 mods (note that ilvl 82 also unlocks T1 elemental resistances, which dilutes the roll pool).
methods.ilvl: 82 for top tiers (75+ is enough for many chase mods)
steps: Perfect Orb of Transmutation: adds one modifier with minimum modifier level 70, so it biases the FIRST mod toward high tiers. This is the tool for landing a high +skill, movement speed, spell damage, resistance or life roll.

Then a Greater or Perfect Orb of Augmentation adds the second mod. Perfect biases higher tiers but is expensive; Greater is far cheaper and still reaches high tiers for many mods, so it is the volume-friendly choice.
Keep magic bases that rolled a desirable prefix + suffix pair (see per-piece combos below). Orb-of-Annulment-spam or re-roll the misses cheaply.
methods.est_cost: a few orbs per base; profit is from volume, not any single roll
methods.mechanics_note: Orb minimum modifier levels: the standard Orb of Transmutation has no minimum (low tiers possible); the Greater Orb is minimum level 55 per the wiki, though a 0.5.0 currency guide reports it was lowered to 44 this patch (unconfirmed, verify on a live source); the Perfect Orb is minimum level 70. Mods whose top tier sits below 70 (for example Increased Rarity, or Spirit on an amulet) still roll under Perfect Transmutation, but only at their highest tier. Note: the Recombinator was removed for the Runes of Aldur league, so 2-mod magic bases, essences and desecration are the main targeted-crafting routes right now.
methods.profit_note: Profitability is economy-dependent and I am not quoting prices. The original transcript's example values (such as +3-skill amulets selling for tens of divines) are a past league snapshot, not current 0.5 numbers. Rank targets by demand and rarity below, then check live trade for the current price. Mod availability and levels are read from CraftPath's own pools.

pieces.ilvl: 75+ for chase mods; 82 for T1 resists
pieces.chase_prefixes: Spirit (tops out around mod level 54); % increased Spell Damage (to ~75); Increased Rarity
pieces.chase_suffixes: +levels to all melee / projectile / spell / minion skills (to ~75), the headline seller; Increased Rarity; a strong attribute or resistance
pieces.top_combos: +skill levels + Spirit; +skill levels + Rarity; Spirit + Rarity; +skill levels + a resistance/attribute
pieces.note: Highest-value flip slot. Perfect Transmute can hit a high +skill suffix directly; Spirit caps below 70 so it rolls at its top tier.

pieces.ilvl: 82 for T1 resists; Iron Ring adds a flat-physical implicit
pieces.chase_prefixes: Adds flat elemental damage to attacks (to ~75); Adds flat physical to attacks; Increased Rarity
pieces.chase_suffixes: High single/double resistance; Attributes; Increased Rarity
pieces.top_combos: Flat added elemental + a resistance; Flat added damage + attribute; Life + a high resistance
pieces.note: No +skills or spirit on rings. Value is flat added damage for attack builds plus clean resistance/attribute rolls.
No attack/cast speed, skills or spirit, so flip value is modest. Volume play: a high life prefix with a strong resistance/attribute suffix.

pieces.ilvl: 82 for T1 resists; Spirit reaches ~mod level 78
pieces.note: Body Spirit goes high, so a Perfect-Transmute spirit roll on a good defence base is a strong flip for spirit-stacking builds.

pieces.chase_suffixes: +levels to all skills (caps low, ~41); High resistance; Crit chance; Increased Rarity
pieces.top_combos: Life + resistance; Rarity + resistance; Life + crit chance
pieces.note: Helmet +skills caps at a low tier, so it is a minor chase. Life + resistance and rarity bases are the reliable flips.

pieces.chase_prefixes: Adds flat elemental/physical to attacks (to ~75); High maximum Life
pieces.chase_suffixes: Attack Speed (to ~60); High resistance; +levels to all skills (caps ~41)
pieces.top_combos: Flat added damage + attack speed; Life + resistance; Flat added damage + resistance
pieces.note: Attack builds pay for flat added damage + attack speed gloves. +skills caps low here.

pieces.ilvl: 82 for T1 resists; Movement Speed reaches mod level 82
pieces.chase_prefixes: Movement Speed (the chase, to 82); High maximum Life
pieces.top_combos: Movement Speed + resistance; Movement Speed + attribute
pieces.note: Movement Speed and Life are both prefixes, so a magic boot carries only one. The universal sellable boot is high Movement Speed + a resistance/attribute suffix.

pieces.chase_prefixes: % increased Physical Damage (to 82); Adds flat physical / elemental to attacks (to ~81)
pieces.chase_suffixes: +levels to all melee/projectile skills (to ~81), the big suffix; Attack Speed; Crit chance
pieces.top_combos: Phys% + +skill levels; Flat added elemental + attack speed; Flat added + crit chance
pieces.note: Weapon +skill levels (to ~81) is a prime Perfect-Transmute target. Pair with a phys% or flat-damage prefix.

pieces.chase_prefixes: % increased Spell Damage (focus, to ~60); flat added spell damage of your element
pieces.chase_suffixes: +levels to all (spell) skills (to ~81 on wand/sceptre); Cast Speed; spell Crit
pieces.top_combos: +spell-skill levels + cast speed; Spell Damage + cast speed; +skill levels + spell crit
pieces.note: +skill levels (suffix) is the headline. Sceptres are additionally valued for Spirit and minion mods on some builds; confirm against the specific base before flipping.

pieces.chase_prefixes: Adds flat physical / elemental to attacks (to ~75)
pieces.top_combos: Flat added damage + attack speed; Flat added damage + crit chance
pieces.note: Pure attack-stat slot for bow builds: flat added damage prefix + an attack-speed or crit suffix.

methods.source: Method from public 0.5 base-flipping transcript; orb levels from poe2wiki + a 0.5.0 currency guide; per-piece mod availability/levels from CraftPath's own pools

methods.title: White-base chancing flip (sell bases to Orb-of-Chance gamblers)
methods.when: You can find or buy clean white bases that correspond to a high-demand unique. Selling the base to gamblers is the low-risk play; chancing it yourself is the high-risk play.
methods.base: A Normal (white), unmodded base of the correct type and item level. Orb of Chance only works on Normal items, and PoE2 has no Orb of Scouring, so a failed chance permanently turns the base into a magic/rare (or destroys it). Gamblers therefore burn a fresh white base per attempt, which is exactly why these bases sell.
methods.ilvl: Match the unique's requirement (e.g. Heavy Belt for Headhunter needs item level 50+)
steps: Identify bases that chance into an in-demand unique (see targets below). Confirm the exact base type and any minimum item level on poe2db before stocking.
Low-risk play: sell clean white bases to gamblers on trade. High-risk play: chance them yourself, accepting that most attempts brick the base into a magic/rare.
Stellar Amulets and Heavy Belts are also strong craft or raw-sell bases, so weigh selling-as-a-chance-base against just crafting them.
methods.mechanics_note: Orb of Chance upgrades a Normal item into a magic, rare, or (rarely) unique of the same base type, or bricks it. Most outcomes are magic/rare, not the unique. Some uniques require a minimum base item level. Odds for chase uniques are tiny (community estimates put Headhunter near 0.3% per attempt, roughly 1 in 300).

targets.note: The +all-attributes implicit base. No consolation uniques, so this is a true mega-gamble. The white base is also valuable to craft or resell, so weigh the opportunity cost before deleting it.

Roughly 0.3% per attempt by community estimate. Consolation heavy-belt uniques include Waistgate and Zerphi's Genesis. Bricks on failure, so each try needs a fresh white base, which is why these bases sell well.

targets.base: Lower-tier targets (various bows, belts, resistance rings)
targets.note: Far higher hit rates than Tier 0 chases and the sane place to start. I have not pinned exact 0.5 targets here because the list shifts; check a maintained chance list for the current set and odds.

methods.profit_note: The chance-target list and unique prices shift with the patch and meta. Treat the targets above as a starting point and verify against a live, maintained chance list (for example Maxroll's 0.5.0 'Best Unique Items to Chance' at maxroll.gg/poe2/meta/best-unique-items-to-chance) and current trade prices before committing.
methods.source: 0.5.0: Maxroll chance list; Game8 / Fextralife Headhunter Heavy Belt; Orb of Chance mechanics from community 0.5 guides

meta_warning: Patch 0.5 Runes of Aldur launched May 29, 2026. The endgame meta is only days old and unsettled: community build guides are still being optimized and several new interactions are unconfirmed. The entries below are common target mods plus a reasonable crafting route, inferred from current guides and stable slot priorities. They are NOT verified best-in-slot. Verify on a current build guide before committing large currency.
